supply_chain.dependencies: A significant portion of merchandise is sourced from outside the U.S., with China as the single largest source of imported merchandise
risks: Trade disputes or changes in tax or trade policy between the U.S. and countries from which merchandise is sourced
Imposition of additional tariffs or duties on imported products
Political or economic uncertainty or instability
Outbreak of pandemics or other illnesses
Congestion disrupting railways, trucking, waterways, and ports
Vendor failures to deliver on commitments

regulatory_risks: Changes in laws or regulations affecting workforce (collective bargaining, minimum wage, advance scheduling, health care mandates, classification of exempt/non-exempt employees, classification of workers as employees vs independent contractors)
Changes in legal or regulatory environment through executive orders
Information security, cybersecurity, and data privacy laws and regulations (including reporting and disclosure regimes)
Environmental, social, and governance regulations (including sustainability and diversity, equity, and inclusion)
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and other anti-bribery laws
Pillar Two framework (OECD global minimum tax)
Tariffs imposed or threatened on China, Mexico, Canada, and other countries

sanction_risks: Trade policies, disputes, or sanctions affecting foreign trade
U.S. tariffs imposed or threatened on China, Mexico, Canada, and other countries
Retaliatory actions by countries subject to tariffs
